highlights: Hub latest: d9c2364 data: MRE Signal Flow Visualization — Hub Signals Page Overhaul standup 2026-02-23

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Morning team. February 23rd checkpoint. We've got the MRE Signal Flow Visualization for Hub Signals Page on deck - 676 tickers flowing through 5 strategies with interactive flowcharts and real-time WebSocket updates. Build is healthy at 942 commits this week, uncommitted changes in Hub only. Key blocker remains J-031 merge which is still pending after 48+ hours. Let's architect this visualization system properly.

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: The graph database schema is the foundation here. We need Neo4j or similar to handle 676 ticker nodes connecting through strategy pipelines with real-time relationship updates. WebSocket infrastructure should batch ticker updates every 500ms to prevent UI thrashing. React component hierarchy needs performance monitoring - we're talking potentially 3,380 DOM nodes if every ticker-strategy combination renders. I'll draft the technical architecture spec focusing on data flow optimization.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: The user experience has to be intuitive despite the complexity. Clickable nodes with detailed modals means we need progressive disclosure - show high-level signal flow first, drill down on demand. Color coding by signal strength, smooth animations for real-time updates, and clear visual hierarchy so users aren't overwhelmed by 676 data points. The interaction model should feel like exploring a living system, not debugging a spreadsheet.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: From an instrumentation perspective, we need real-time measurement accuracy across all 676 ticker streams. WebSocket message queuing with failover redundancy, latency monitoring under 100ms for signal updates, and data validation pipelines to catch corrupted ticker feeds. The modal details need to show signal confidence intervals and measurement timestamps. I'll architect the data acquisition layer with proper error handling and reconnection logic.

transcript.speaker: Chris Vermeullen
transcript.message: The financial context matters - these 676 tickers represent significant market coverage across 5 proven strategies. Users need to see not just signal flow but performance attribution and risk metrics within each modal. The visualization should highlight which strategies are generating alpha and which tickers are driving the most P&L impact. We should instrument click-through rates on different nodes to understand what signals traders actually care about.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good alignment on the technical foundation. Elon, your performance concerns about 3,380 DOM nodes are valid - we'll need virtualization.

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: Exactly. React virtualization with intersection observers. Only render visible nodes in the viewport.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: The progressive disclosure model Elon mentioned is critical. Don't show everything at once - that's a recipe for user paralysis.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: Steve's right, but we can't sacrifice data integrity for simplicity. The underlying measurement pipeline needs to capture everything even if the UI filters it.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Perfect. Summary: We're building a performance-monitored React visualization with graph database backend, WebSocket real-time updates, and progressive disclosure UI. Top priorities: technical architecture spec, component hierarchy design, and WebSocket infrastructure planning. This is complex but achievable with proper layering.

actionItems.text: Draft technical architecture specification for MRE Signal Flow Visualization
Design component hierarchy and user interaction flow for signal visualization
Architect WebSocket data acquisition layer with redundancy
Review and approve signal flow visualization requirements
Define performance attribution metrics for strategy visualization

topPriorities: Graph database schema architecture for 676 tickers and 5 strategies
Progressive disclosure UI component hierarchy design

ceoDirectives.directive: What is Lever's direction? → Hybrid — personal tool now with product optionality built in
Should faith content integrate into the Family tab? → Keep faith content as separate standalone section
Deploy idle cash ($75,340)? → No action — keep cash liquid
How deep should standalone faith content go? → Medium build: 15-20 lessons with 2-3 per dimension
What is the minimum viable scope for the Lever App Store submission? → Full current build: all 4 tabs (Finance, Faith, Family, Compass) as-is with 7 faith lessons
Should we unify the dual Supabase instances (Hub vs Website) this sprint or defer? → Defer until after App Store submission — live with manual sync for now
Do we have a privacy policy and terms of service URL ready for the App Store submission? → Need to draft and host tonight — use a simple template
Should we define a paid tier structure before or after the App Store launch? → Launch free but have the paid tier designed and ready to toggle on

Should we prioritize drafting the privacy policy and App Store listing assets (screenshots, description, keywords) THIS sprint as the critical path to submission, or defer to tomorrow? → Ship privacy policy template and draft listing assets tonight
Should we submit the App Store listing as soon as the draft is ready, or do you want a review pass before we hit submit? → Queue draft for Joshua review before submitting
Have you reviewed the App Store listing draft (A-015)? The build is ready to package and submit the moment you approve. → Approve listing as-is and greenlight submission

Once the App Store submission goes through and enters review, should we immediately begin instrumenting analytics (A-020) or prioritize expanding faith lessons to 15-20 (A-013)? → Analytics instrumentation first — we need data before expanding content
Should we set a target date for enabling the paid tier in the Lever app? → Defer decision until we see initial download data
Should we unify the Compass UI across website and mobile app before users encounter both, or keep them separate for now? → Defer until we see which surface gets more usage via analytics

When the app clears review, should we do a soft launch (no promotion, organic only) or immediately push it through newsletter and podcast? → Medium push — announce via newsletter and one podcast mention
When Apple approves the app, should the launch newsletter go out same-day or be scheduled for the following morning? → Same-day if approved before 2pm, otherwise next morning
What is your week-one download target for the Lever app to calibrate launch promotion intensity? → 150 downloads (modest organic + newsletter push)
Which newsletter subject line style do you prefer for the launch announcement? → Story-driven (e.g. 'I built an app. Here's why.')
Should the empty state patches (Family + Compass tabs) block the launch newsletter push, or ship as a same-day post-launch update? → Launch as-is, ship empty state patch as day-one update

Should we pre-schedule the launch newsletter in the CMS (ready to send with one click) or keep it as a manual send so you control exact timing? → Pre-schedule but require Joshua's Slack confirmation before auto-send

The launch newsletter is being pre-scheduled in the CMS tonight per your directive. Do you want to review it in the morning via Slack screenshot, or do a live CMS preview before arming the send? → Slack screenshot review (faster, async)

Once all overnight tasks close, should we shift sprint focus to content expansion (faith lessons 8-15) or technical debt (7 TODOs/FIXMEs, dual Supabase, content sync automation)? → Split — half the team on content, half on tech debt
Should we add a lightweight welcome modal (3 sentences, no tutorial) to the day-one post-launch patch? → Yes — add welcome modal to the day-one patch
How should Joshua allocate the 2-hour build window this morning? → Newsletter screenshot review first (2 min), then crash reporting check (5 min), then analytics scaffolding (remaining time)

If Apple approves the app today (Friday), do you want to launch-push today or hold for Monday morning for better newsletter open rates? → Same-day push but schedule a follow-up Monday reminder newsletter
Did you complete the three morning tasks: (1) newsletter screenshot review/approval, (2) crash reporting verification, (3) analytics scaffolding? → Newsletter + crash done, analytics not started

Elon and James want to stage the analytics Swift hooks PR so you only need to review and merge (5 min) instead of coding it yourself (~60 min). Approve this approach? → Yes — let them stage it, I'll review and merge
Should Alex draft the Monday follow-up reminder newsletter now or wait until we see initial download numbers after launch? → Draft a generic version now and personalize with data Sunday night

The analytics Swift hooks PR will be staged and validated tonight. Do you want to review-and-merge it tonight (enabling a fully loaded day-one patch) or queue it for your morning window? → Merge tonight but only if James's validation passes clean

We now have 10 faith lessons drafted or in review (7 shipped + lessons 8, 9, 10 in pipeline). Should we push to hit 15 lessons before launch promotion, or is 10 sufficient for launch with the rest added post-launch? → Launch at 10, commit to shipping 15 within first week post-launch
Jett's vaccine appointment was scheduled for today (Feb 20). Can you confirm it was completed? → Yes, completed — close both tickets

Should tonight's build session prioritize drafting the Family tab feature spec (expanding from 455 lines) or stay exclusively focused on launch-critical items? → Family tab spec is a priority — assign it now

The analytics Swift hooks PR is being validated tonight. If James's tests pass clean, do you want to do your review-and-merge tonight as previously directed, or has your availability changed? → Yes, I'll review tonight — ping me on Slack when validation passes

Cash has grown to $87,263 (up from $75,340 when you last said keep liquid). Fear & Greed is 42 (fear), SPY regime is bull. Should we deploy any of it? → Evaluate specific deployment plan and decide tomorrow with more data

J-021 (day-one patch) and J-024 (analytics PR) have been staged and validated for over 24 hours. Can you commit to merging both in the first 10 minutes of your build window today? → Yes — merge both first thing
Can you confirm J-021 and J-024 merges will happen before 2pm PT today, or should we shift the launch pipeline to Monday morning? → Merge both before 2pm today

The $87K cash evaluation was deferred yesterday with 'decide tomorrow with more data.' Fear & Greed is 42 (fear), SPY regime is bull, zero buy signals, 24 holds. Deploy or continue holding? → Continue holding — keep fully liquid until post-launch revenue data

J-021 and J-024 have been staged and validated for 48+ hours. Can you commit to merging both tomorrow (Sunday) morning, or should we formally reset the launch pipeline to a Tuesday target? → team recommendation

The welcome modal was a CEO directive for the day-one patch, but the day-one patch itself is blocked behind the initial submission merge. Should the modal copy be drafted tonight so it's ready, or deprioritize entirely? → team recommendation
Approve the welcome modal design direction: single screen, sunrise gradient, three pillar cards (Faith/Family/Finance), 'Let's go' CTA, no carousel or tutorial? → Approve as designed
Should the finance card include a subtle 'Pro' label hint at premium features, or keep the first-launch modal entirely free-tier? → Include subtle Pro label on finance card
Which fixed scripture line should anchor the faith card preview on the welcome modal? → Defer scripture selection to Peterson and Alex with no CEO review needed

After merging J-031, should we submit to Apple immediately with the current build, or hold submission to include the welcome modal in the initial binary? → Submit immediately after merge, ship modal as day-one update

You committed to hitting 15 faith lessons within the first week post-launch. Should agents begin drafting lessons 11-15 now (pre-launch) or wait until after submission? → Begin drafting lessons 11-15 now in parallel with submission
Should the newsletter timing decision (J-007) be made now with contingency plans, or wait until we see Apple's actual review timeline after submission? → team recommendation
Should we prepare both same-day and next-morning newsletter templates, or commit to one timing strategy now? → team recommendation
What scope should the paper trading MVP launch with - manual portfolio entry (6 weeks) or full automated brokerage sync (12 weeks)? → Manual entry MVP for faster launch
Should paper trading be positioned as a free feature to drive adoption or premium feature to generate revenue? → Basic free, advanced analytics premium
How does paper trading fit with Lever's current finance-faith-family positioning - expand scope or separate product? → Integrate into current Lever app as expanded finance tab
Should we decide newsletter timing (J-007) now with contingency plans, or wait until Apple's actual review timeline after submission? → team recommendation
Should paper trading include educational prompts about real money psychology differences as Jordan suggested? → Ship clean first then add education layer
